baseline.note: main; includes opp-prior, Boss/Wally KO-gate, Ignition fix, tutor-zone, no-suicide-scope, develop-before-attack, dig-for-line, thin-END veto, Ignition-waste veto

isolation: candidates implemented as default-off STARMIE_VARIANT toggles, committed to branch exp/starmie-heuristic-lab-v3 (NOT main), per stop conditions
screen: imitation 24 games, process-isolated (low-frequency rules -> aggregate within noise, cannot rank): BASELINE 57.8, retreat_guard 58.0, hammer_threshold 56.9, cape_conditional 57.0

retreat_guard_v1.rule: do not retreat Mega Starmie (best attacker/tank) without a benched ready Mega replacement
retreat_guard_v1.mechanism: sound -- targets the promotion/transition failure seen in losses
retreat_guard_v1.recommendation: keep as toggle; plausibly helpful; needs a field A/B / ladder to verify (low frequency)

hammer_threshold_v1.rule: play Crushing Hammer only when opp Active energy in 1-2 band AND Active HP>=100 (vs any>=1)
hammer_threshold_v1.mechanism: matches the dominant pilot band (Hammer inventory: 57% on 1-energy actives) but loses 0-energy bench-target and 3+ cases; Hammer is a coin flip
hammer_threshold_v1.recommendation: broad rule matches pilots slightly better; threshold is a reasonable but unproven narrowing -> baseline for now

cape_conditional_v1.rule: play Heros Cape only when Mega Starmie remaining HP<=230 (damaged enough that +100 matters)
cape_conditional_v1.mechanism: matches dominant pilot pattern (Cape inventory: 58% on Megas<=165) but loses the 19% proactive full-HP capes
cape_conditional_v1.recommendation: plausible (do not waste the 1-of ACE SPEC early); unproven -> keep as toggle

OVERALL: The three rule candidates are MINOR (low-frequency, within-noise on imitation). The high-value finding is the eval.py audit: the search LEAF is deck-agnostic and cannot prefer a Mega Starmie attacker over Cinderace -- the search-level ROOT of the Turbo-Flare-loop / 0-prize losses. The concrete improvements that matter most this round are the committed BUG FIXES (dig-for-line, thin-END veto, Ignition-waste veto) which directly change the bad ladder behavior. Recommended next: a Starmie-aware leaf ATTACK-READINESS term (separate isolated experiment).
submission_recommendation: a fresh submission is warranted from main (359f9ae) -- it has the real bug fixes the ladder build lacks; but that is a MANUAL decision (no auto-build).
